questions.question: Explain the CUDA thread hierarchy (threads, blocks, grids) and how thread indexing works using built-in variables.
keyPoints: Threads are the smallest unit; blocks group threads on the same SM; grids are all blocks from one kernel launch
threadIdx, blockIdx, blockDim, gridDim are built-in variables for computing unique thread indices
Global 1D index = blockIdx.x * blockDim.x + threadIdx.x; 2D/3D use .y and .z components

questions.question: Discuss the warp execution model in CUDA. Explain what warps are, how warp divergence affects performance, and how occupancy impacts kernel efficiency. Include strategies for optimization.
keyPoints: A warp is 32 consecutive threads that execute in SIMT lockstep
Warp divergence serializes execution of different branches, reducing throughput
Occupancy is the ratio of active warps to maximum warps on an SM
Occupancy is limited by register usage, shared memory, and block size
Optimization strategies: use block sizes that are multiples of 32, minimize divergence by branching on block/warp level, use grid-stride loops for large datasets
